era: 1990-present. Grunge. E=7. Seattle. Maverick Records. Kevin Martin vocals. Classic-rock inheritance. 4 million debut. Commercial success without critical establishment acceptance. Long career.
key_albums: Candlebox (1993, Maverick) -- self-titled debut; 4 million US copies; You, Far Behind, Cover Me, Change
Lucy (1995, Maverick) -- follow-up; ~500K copies; better critical reception, less commercial
Happy Pills (1998, Maverick) -- third album; commercial momentum further reduced
Into the Sun (2008, Sire) -- reunion album after 2000-2006 hiatus
Disappearing in Airports (2016, E1) -- fifth studio album

theory_nodes.concept: MAVERICK RECORDS PARADOX. Maverick was founded by Madonna in 1992, distributed by Time Warner. Grunge defined itself against the mainstream pop industry; Candlebox signing to a pop megastar's label and achieving 4 million in sales is the commercial scenario grunge's cultural identity resisted. The engine documents the paradox without resolving it.
theory_nodes.description: The Maverick paradox: grunge success on a label founded by the pop mainstream.

theory_nodes.concept: COMMERCIAL SUCCESS AS CRITICAL DISQUALIFICATION. Candlebox's 4 million debut outsold most of the grunge canon's critically celebrated records (Mudhoney, TAD, Hammerbox, Love Battery). The Seattle critical establishment applied a logic of authenticity that disqualified commercial success as evidence against grunge purity. The engine does not apply this logic.
theory_nodes.description: 4 million copies as critical evidence against authenticity: the paradox of commercial grunge success.

theory_nodes.concept: CLASSIC-ROCK VOCAL INHERITANCE. Kevin Martin's voice draws more from Robert Plant and Paul Rodgers than from the punk-metal lineage of Cobain, Cornell, or Staley. This inheritance is what separated Candlebox from their Seattle peers and enabled dual accessibility: heavy enough for the grunge audience, melodic enough for classic-rock FM listeners.
theory_nodes.description: Classic-rock vs. post-punk vocal roots: the acoustic basis for Candlebox's commercial reach.

theory_nodes.concept: LONG CAREER PATTERN. Candlebox has maintained activity through two hiatuses across 35+ years. Five studio albums. The peak commercial entry (1993) was followed by committed long-term activity at reduced commercial scale. This is the pattern for most grunge-era acts: the commercial window closed but the band continued.
theory_nodes.description: Post-commercial-window persistence: the pattern of grunge bands continuing after the commercial moment.

cross_references.relationship: E=7 consecutive. Post-summit cooldown holds. Memorial record (317) vs. commercial record (318): both E=7 from different angles of peripherality from the critical canon.

6M sales vs. 4M sales. Both multi-million grunge records. Critical reception inverted: Dirt is canonical (produced under duress, Layne Staley's heroin context), Candlebox is not (classic-rock roots, Maverick label). Commercial tier comparable, critical status opposite.
13M sales vs. 4M sales. Ten is the commercial ceiling of the grunge canon. Candlebox is the next commercial tier.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=5 SPS + E=7 S36. Both operating below their genre's visible surface: Candlebox below the critical canon; the Cascadia subduction below the ocean floor. The infrasonic deep current vs. the classic-rock grunge commercial current.
